jokes.text: Come on, C, you're not big.
jokes.setup: Carlton says he's sick of being a big loser
jokes.punchline: Will takes it literally - Carlton isn't big (he's short)
jokes.explanation: Classic Will deflecting Carlton's self-pity with a height joke. Well-constructed but predictable.

jokes.text: You wouldn't believe the money to be made kicking people out of their own homes.
jokes.setup: Carlton mentions attending a foreclosure seminar
jokes.punchline: He frames evicting families as a cheerful business opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton - obliviously cruel capitalism delivered with naive enthusiasm. Dark comedy that fits his character perfectly.

jokes.text: Such a big heart for such a little man.
jokes.setup: Carlton just talked about profiting from foreclosures
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically calls it big-hearted while emphasizing Carlton's small stature
jokes.explanation: Clean ironic response with another height dig. Effective but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: Ah. Fore. Uhn.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asks who could replace Carlton as golf partner
jokes.punchline: Will makes golf sounds while miming a swing to volunteer himself
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy bit showing Will's enthusiasm. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Oh, me? No... Yes.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asks if Will would like to play golf
jokes.punchline: Will's quick reversal from modest refusal to eager acceptance
jokes.explanation: Will's enthusiasm overriding his false modesty. Simple but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Well, that's not exactly all we think about, if you know what I'm talking about. Ha.
jokes.setup: Ashley says all men think about is winning
jokes.punchline: Will makes a sexual innuendo with obvious wink-nudge delivery
jokes.explanation: Generic sexual innuendo that's predictable and relies on obvious setup. Weak writing.

jokes.text: Of course, we don't have losing to compare it to.
jokes.setup: Vivian says they enter tennis tournaments just for fun, winning doesn't make it more fun
jokes.punchline: She admits they've never lost so they don't actually know
jokes.explanation: Nice subversion - undermines her point about not caring about winning by revealing they always win.

jokes.text: You go, girl.
jokes.setup: Vivian just made a clever point about always winning
jokes.punchline: Ashley's enthusiastic support using 90s slang
jokes.explanation: Period-appropriate slang reaction. Functional but relies on catchphrase humor.

jokes.text: Oh, goody. I thought I was going to run out of things to do today.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey sees Hilary has made a mess of the room
jokes.punchline: He sarcastically expresses joy at having more work to do
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey dry sarcasm about his thankless job. Well-delivered character comedy.

jokes.text: Miss Hilary, it's comments like that, that makes this job worthwhile.
jokes.setup: Hilary calls the place a mess right in front of Geoffrey
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey sarcastically says her insults make the job worthwhile
jokes.explanation: Another perfect Geoffrey moment - passive-aggressive sarcasm delivered with butler politeness.

jokes.text: Ah. Another pair of hands to lighten the load.
jokes.setup: Hilary mentions Regis Philbin is coming over
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey sarcastically suggests a TV host will help with housework
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's dry wit continues. Good economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Hey, what's up, Regis? / You can call me Mr. Philbin.
jokes.setup: Will casually greets the famous TV host by first name
jokes.punchline: Regis immediately corrects him to use formal address
jokes.explanation: Classic culture clash - Will's casual West Philly style meets show business formality.

jokes.text: That's a joke. / I got him.
jokes.setup: Regis corrects Will's informality
jokes.punchline: Regis reveals he was kidding and Will pretends he knew all along
jokes.explanation: Mild misdirection joke with Will trying to save face. Functional but not remarkable.

jokes.text: Hey, don't ever call me Reg.
jokes.setup: Will calls him Reg after receiving golf advice
jokes.punchline: Regis seriously tells him not to use that nickname
jokes.explanation: Sets up the running gag about his name. Simple but effective establishment.

jokes.text: No, I'm serious. Don't ever call me Reg.
jokes.setup: Will calls him Reg again, thinking it's funny
jokes.punchline: Regis seriously repeats that he doesn't like the nickname
jokes.explanation: Escalates the running gag by having Regis get more serious about it.

jokes.text: Well, if it isn't Martina and Steffi.
jokes.setup: Vivian and Ashley return from tennis practice
jokes.punchline: Will compares them to famous tennis champions Navratilova and Graf
jokes.explanation: Topical sports reference that fits Will's personality. Good economy but relies on name-dropping.

jokes.text: 'Black number five coming up. Ha-ha-ha.'
jokes.setup: Ashley mentions winning with 'black number five coming up'
jokes.punchline: Will imitates Uncle Phil's celebration dance mockingly
jokes.explanation: Will making fun of Uncle Phil's victory celebration. Physical comedy that works better visually.

jokes.text: And I'm gonna pretend I ain't see that little dance you was just doing.
jokes.setup: Will catches Uncle Phil doing a victory dance
jokes.punchline: Will decides to pretend he didn't see Phil's embarrassing moment
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Will being kind by ignoring Uncle Phil's dorky celebration while still getting the laugh.

jokes.text: What? A sandwich? Uncle Phil, we just had breakfast a half hour ago.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil mentions needing to get a sand wedge
jokes.punchline: Will thinks he's talking about food, not a golf club
jokes.explanation: Classic misunderstanding joke based on golf terminology. Predictable but well-executed.

jokes.text: Yeah, I know. Turkey, bacon, three layers of white toast.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil corrects that it's a golf club, not food
jokes.punchline: Will doubles down, describing a specific sandwich
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the misunderstanding with specific food details that make it funnier.

jokes.text: Oh! Hey, Mercer, you should save that dazzling wit for the courtroom. You might even win a case.
jokes.setup: Mercer insults Phil about needing someone else to swing his clubs
jokes.punchline: Phil suggests Mercer's weak humor might help him win cases (implying he usually loses)
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback from Phil that works on multiple levels - questioning both Mercer's humor and legal skills.

jokes.text: I hate to be a stickler, but that's my chair.
jokes.setup: Hilary sits in what appears to be a normal chair on set
jokes.punchline: Regis reveals he's territorial about his specific chair
jokes.explanation: Shows Regis as petty about small things. Sets up the dynamic between him and Hilary.

jokes.text: It would be in my best interest if I could sit in the same chair I've been sitting in for the last 15 years.
jokes.setup: Hilary wants to sit on her 'good side'
jokes.punchline: Regis emphasizes his long tenure and routine
jokes.explanation: Escalates the chair conflict by emphasizing Regis's seniority. Mild workplace tension comedy.

jokes.text: Fine. Big baby.
jokes.setup: Regis insists on his chair
jokes.punchline: Hilary calls him a big baby under her breath
jokes.explanation: Simple insult that establishes Hilary's frustration. Basic but effective character moment.

jokes.text: I'm drawing a blank here. / Don't fix it. / Fix what? / It.
jokes.setup: Regis starts the saying 'If it ain't broke...'
jokes.punchline: Hilary doesn't know the phrase, leading to confused back-and-forth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - her cluelessness about common sayings creates genuine confusion. Well-structured escalating misunderstanding.

jokes.text: Big baby.
jokes.setup: Regis suggests skipping banter altogether
jokes.punchline: Hilary repeats her earlier insult
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier insult. Simple repetition that works as a button on the scene.

jokes.text: Hell, no, how could we? He's an even worse golfer than you.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks if they won their golf round
jokes.punchline: Phil insults Will's golf skills while explaining their loss
jokes.explanation: Phil's blunt assessment of Will's golf skills. Good character moment showing their dynamic.

jokes.text: You had a great time losing?
jokes.setup: Phil and Will are happy despite losing
jokes.punchline: Carlton can't understand enjoying a loss
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton response - his competitive nature can't comprehend fun without winning.

jokes.text: I mean, once the police come and toss the family out, it's mostly paperwork.
jokes.setup: Carlton considers missing his foreclosure seminar to play golf
jokes.punchline: He casually describes evictions as routine paperwork
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Carlton callback - his casual cruelty about homelessness is both shocking and perfectly in character. Dark comedy gold.

jokes.text: Let me tell you, this dude right here is just straight loqued-out on a golf course, man.
jokes.setup: Phil wants to praise Will's golf skills
jokes.punchline: Phil tries to use street slang but gets it wrong ('loqued-out')
jokes.explanation: Classic Uncle Phil trying to be cool with made-up slang. Perfect character comedy showing his attempts to connect with Will.

jokes.text: All you gotta do is not concentrate.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asks Will to show him how to miss easy putts
jokes.punchline: Will gives the paradoxical advice to 'not concentrate'
jokes.explanation: Clever paradox - the instruction to not concentrate is itself something you'd have to concentrate on. Good absurd logic.

jokes.text: I can not concentrate.
jokes.setup: Will says not concentrating is the key to missing putts
jokes.punchline: Phil declares he can indeed not concentrate (emphasis on 'can not')
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the concentration paradox with wordplay on can/cannot.

jokes.text: Yes, but I'm still working here.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks if Geoffrey ever felt stupid about doing something he thought was right
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey implies working for the Banks family was one such mistake
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey moment - dry, self-deprecating, and subtly insulting his employers. Excellent character comedy with great economy.

jokes.text: Well, you shouldn't. Not because of that anyway.
jokes.setup: Carlton says he feels like an idiot for missing the golf tournament
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey says he shouldn't feel like an idiot for that (implying other reasons)
jokes.explanation: Another excellent Geoffrey burn - consoling Carlton while simultaneously insulting him. Perfect butler politeness masking cruelty.

jokes.text: Bet it was that dirty boy Bobby Mercer. You know, it's always the quiet ones.
jokes.setup: Will's golf clubs have been destroyed
jokes.punchline: Will suspects the opponent's son and makes a generalization about quiet people
jokes.explanation: Standard 'quiet ones' joke with mild paranoia. Functional but relies on cliché.

jokes.text: Wait, Carl...? What, I'm supposed to play on my knees?
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil suggests Will can play with Carlton's clubs
jokes.punchline: Will realizes Carlton's clubs are too short for his height
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy about the height difference. Works well as physical humor setup.

jokes.text: Maybe worse.
jokes.setup: Will says he can play just as bad with new clubs
jokes.punchline: He immediately ups the ante by saying maybe worse
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating escalation. Simple but effective character moment.

jokes.text: Shoot, with my swing, I might as well play with these.
jokes.setup: Will is holding broken golf club pieces
jokes.punchline: He jokes that broken clubs match his terrible swing
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about his golf skills. Visual gag works well with the self-deprecation.

jokes.text: It takes two people to banter. / Not when I do it.
jokes.setup: Regis says it takes two people to banter
jokes.punchline: Hilary claims she can banter solo
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary line - completely missing the point of what banter is while being confidently wrong. Excellent character comedy.

jokes.text: When that little round yellow fuzzy thing comes at you, take a swipe at it.
jokes.setup: Ashley can't hear the tennis ball coming
jokes.punchline: Vivian describes a tennis ball in the most basic terms possible
jokes.explanation: Mildly funny description of a tennis ball as if Ashley doesn't know what it is.

jokes.text: Your outfit was too loud.
jokes.setup: Ashley says she couldn't hear the ball coming
jokes.punchline: She blames her mother's loud (visually) outfit for her inability to hear
jokes.explanation: Clever play on 'loud' meaning both auditory and visual. Good wordplay with nice economy.

jokes.text: But if you double fault one more time, I am going to put you up for adoption.
jokes.setup: Vivian says she loves Ashley despite her tennis mistakes
jokes.punchline: She threatens adoption if Ashley makes another error
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation from loving mother to abandonment threat. Good contrast between setup and punchline.

jokes.text: Great, maybe then I'll wind up with a mother who can hit a decent backhand.
jokes.setup: Vivian threatens to put Ashley up for adoption
jokes.punchline: Ashley counters that maybe she'd get a better tennis-playing mother
jokes.explanation: Excellent comeback that escalates the tennis competition conflict. Ashley holds her own in the argument.

jokes.text: Well, let me say it louder then.
jokes.setup: Vivian says she'll pretend she didn't hear the backhand insult
jokes.punchline: Ashley offers to repeat it louder
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the mother-daughter tennis argument. Ashley continues to hold her ground.

jokes.text: As long as she ain't got a forehead like her daddy.
jokes.setup: Mr. Jenkins wants Will to meet his daughter
jokes.punchline: Will makes a crack about Jenkins' prominent forehead
jokes.explanation: Standard appearance-based humor that fits Will's character but is somewhat shallow.

jokes.text: He told me he didn't have any children.
jokes.setup: Jenkins walks away after Will insults his forehead
jokes.punchline: Will realizes Jenkins was offended and is now denying his daughter
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the forehead joke showing consequences. Will realizes he went too far.

jokes.text: Carlton, you still owe me for those Milk Duds.
jokes.setup: Rich tells Will his money is no good and to put it away
jokes.punchline: But he still wants Carlton to pay for candy
jokes.explanation: Nice contrast between treating Will generously but still wanting Carlton's small debt. Shows different treatment.

jokes.text: Ha-ha-ha. You keep talking, Mercer, and I'm gonna hand you something else.
jokes.setup: Mercer suggests Phil just hand over the trophy without playing
jokes.punchline: Phil threatens physical retaliation with vague menace
jokes.explanation: Phil's competitive anger coming through. Generic threat but fits the rivalry.

jokes.text: I mean... Ow! My shoulder string.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil questions how Will pulled a hamstring playing golf
jokes.punchline: Will changes it to 'shoulder string' - inventing body parts
jokes.explanation: Will making up anatomy to maintain his fake injury. Good wordplay and character moment.

jokes.text: If you help me win this thing, you'll never have to work another day in your life. But you have fun.
jokes.setup: Phil repeats his earlier promise to Will about never working
jokes.punchline: He adds 'But you have fun' as an afterthought, showing his priorities
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Phil's competitive nature winning over his lessons about fun. Character development moment.

jokes.text: Grab your club, son. If you help me win this thing... you'll never have to work another day in your life. But you have fun.
jokes.setup: Phil gives Carlton the same speech he gave Will earlier
jokes.punchline: The addition of 'But you have fun' shows he's learned from the earlier conversation
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Phil's character growth and understanding of what Carlton wants.

jokes.text: Regis loves me for finally bringing a little flavor to the show.
jokes.setup: The show clearly went badly with Regis and Hilary fighting
jokes.punchline: Hilary completely misreads the situation as success
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary delusion - completely misunderstanding what happened. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: That's right, Reg, I'll be right here next to you all week.
jokes.setup: We expect to see Hilary announced as the co-host
jokes.punchline: It's actually someone else entirely, and she calls him 'Reg'
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic ending - Hilary's replacement does exactly what Regis hated (calling him Reg) but he accepts it. Good callback structure.

jokes.text: Oh, great. Now where am I supposed to sit?
jokes.setup: Hilary realizes she's been replaced on the show
jokes.punchline: She's still focused on the chair issue from earlier
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback and perfect ending - Hilary completely misses the point of being fired and focuses on seating. Excellent character consistency.
